% Auto-generated by generate_table.py (compact pilot layout). Regenerate:
%   python3 generate_table.py --dim 1000
\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Mean best fitness on the CEC~2013 LSGO benchmark at $D = 10^{3}$ after $3 \times 10^{6}$ function evaluations with differential evolution (DE). Rows are benchmark functions $F_{1}$--$F_{15}$; columns are subspace maps with assignment mode; the leftmost column is the full space baseline with fixed $F$ and $CR$. Dual EA LoRA columns with additive assignment at ranks $r \in \{1, 2, 4, 8\}$ follow immediately after the baseline. Column \emph{v2} at $r = 1$ uses the elite handoff in \cref{alg:lsgo-dual-ea} (lines 279--281). Earlier $r = 1$ runs without \emph{v2} used unconditional replacement of the worst full space member. Blockwise Dual EA LoRA variants at $B = 10$ and $r = 1$ follow (IB, S, GS, Diag, and R1 in \cref{tab:lsgo-lora-compression-comparison}). Single phase columns then list RandProj, RandBlock, and LoRA at $d \in \{10, 50\}$ or $r {=} 8$ in both assignment modes where applicable, even when runs are not yet complete. The $d_{\mathrm{search}}$ row lists the optimizer search dimension logged for each configuration (problem dimension $D$ for full space, subspace dimension otherwise). Each entry is the mean of final best fitness averaged over three algorithmic seed runs. Lower is better. Bold entries are the best mean in each row. Superscript $\dag$, $\ddag$, and $\approx$ on an entry indicate that the method is significantly better than, significantly worse than, or not significantly different from the leftmost full space baseline (fixed $F$ and $CR$) on that function (Wilcoxon rank-sum, $\alpha = 0.05$). The bottom row gives aggregate win/tie/loss counts from the same tests. Missing cells indicate missing runs.}
\label{tab:lsgo-preliminary}

\sisetup{mode=text, reset-text-series=false, detect-weight=true}%
